frameworks.name: optimal evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian-optimal integration of noisy evidence over an adapted timescale.

frameworks.name: dynamic-environment inference
frameworks.one_line: Extends static accumulator models by adding optimal discounting of stale evidence.

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion / bounded accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Classical static accumulator framework that the paper builds on and generalizes.

core_question: Can rats adapt the timescale over which they accumulate and discount sensory evidence to match a changing environment?
key_assumptions: optimal timescale jointly depends on stimulus volatility and sensory noise
decisions arise from accumulated evidence with leak matched to environment statistics
rats can be modeled as near-optimal Bayesian inference agents
manipulations: environment dynamics (volatility / switch rate of the latent state)
stimulus statistics setting the optimal discounting timescale
key_findings: rats accumulated and discounted evidence near-optimally when sensory noise was accounted for
experimenter-controlled environment dynamics shifted rats between short and long accumulation timescales
the optimal timescale depends jointly on stimulus statistics and sensory noise
interpretations: rodents flexibly adapt their evidence-accumulation timescale to environmental volatility
a unified normative account links static and dynamic accumulation regimes and predicts changes of mind
